title: vitis coignetiae f. glabrescens
intro: Vitis coignetiae f. glabrescens, also known as Japanese creeper or Japanese grapevine, is a deciduous vine native to East Asia, characterized by its woody stem and thorny branches. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, with medicinal properties attributed to its high content of flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vitis coignetiae f. glabrescens include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing potential anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies to enhance its medicinal properties.
